title: turraea humilis
intro: Turraea humilis, also known as the West African turraea or African boxwood, is a plant species that belongs to the family Meliaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of respiratory infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Turraea humilis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties, and its bark and roots are often used in traditional remedies.
